title: One-Shot Bodies, Streaming, Uploads, Decompression, and Resource Limits · Go Client Systems · Mastery Check
summary: A release board evaluating a changed infrastructure-provider contract must defend independent decisions across fresh cases, including one ambiguous failure and one changed boundary for one-shot bodies, streaming, uploads, decompression, and resource limits, producing bounded streaming body pipeline. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a Go 1.26 net/http bounded streaming body pipeline. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice,…
objectives: Treat request and response bodies as owned one-shot streams with explicit read, close, replay, and cleanup rules. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Limit compressed and decoded response reads before allocation, parsing, logging, or persistence. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Stream large uploads with known ownership, backpressure, progress, cancellation, and retryability. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go One Shot Body Ownership · changed case · Quiz One Shot Body Ownership
steps.instruction:
quiz case — Go vendor-release candidate streams a 16 MiB upload and receives a 40 KiB compressed body that expands past 40 MiB. Define one-shot ownership, replay, compressed and decoded limits, cancellation, trailers, and cleanup.
